notes: Roman philosopher and statesman, author of The Consolation of Philosophy, executed by King Theoderic.

docs.title: Letter 1045
docs.author: Cassiodorus
docs.subject_summary: Book 1, Letter XLV: BOETHIO V. I. PATRICIO
docs.excerpt: We have learned that you, nourished by extensive learning, know these arts so well that while the uneducated practice them mechanically, you have drunk from the very fountain of the disciplines.

docs.title: Letter 8036
docs.author: Ennodius of Pavia
docs.excerpt: … others take for granted: that your intellectual gifts are a public good, not merely a private accomplishment. May they continue to serve both you and the world. Farewell.
